data.title: Lendlease Director Increases Holding via Distribution Reinvestment Plan
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Lendlease Group ( (AU:LLC) ) has provided an update.

Lendlease Group has disclosed a minor change in director Lianne Buck’s holding of ordinary stapled securities, following an issue of additional units under the company’s Distribution Reinvestment Plan. Buck’s direct interest increased from 6,000 to 6,096 stapled securities after acquiring 96 securities at $3.8499 each, with no disposals reported.

The transaction reflects routine participation by a board member in Lendlease’s reinvestment scheme rather than a strategic shift in ownership. Such incremental changes are typically viewed as standard governance disclosures to the market, offering transparency on director alignment with shareholders without signaling any significant change in the company’s operational or strategic outlook.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:LLC) stock is a Sell with a A$3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lendlease Group stock, see the AU:LLC Stock Forecast page.

More about Lendlease Group

Lendlease Group is an international property and infrastructure company that operates through Lendlease Corporation Limited and Lendlease Trust. It focuses on developing, owning and managing real estate and infrastructure assets, with its securities listed as ordinary stapled securities on the Australian Securities Exchange, giving investors exposure to both the corporation and the trust.

Average Trading Volume: 1,980,473

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$2.38B

Yuanda China Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2789) ) just unveiled an update.

Yuanda China Holdings Limited has scheduled a board meeting for 31 March 2026 to review and potentially approve the audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025. The Hong Kong-listed company, which operates through subsidiaries in mainland China, will also consider the recommendation of a final dividend, a decision that could signal management’s view on profitability and cash flow.

The board will additionally address any other unspecified business matters at the meeting, underscoring a routine but important point in the company’s corporate calendar. The outcome of the session, particularly regarding the annual results and dividend, will be closely watched by shareholders for indications of operating performance and capital-return policy.

Yuanda China Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2789. The group operates through subsidiaries in mainland China, though the specific industry and product focus are not detailed in this announcement. Its governance structure includes executive and independent non-executive directors based in the PRC.

data.title: Argent BioPharma Clears Secondary Trading for New Share Issue
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Argent Biopharma ( (AU:RGT) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Argent BioPharma has notified investors of the issue of 1,250,000 fully paid ordinary shares, confirming that the securities were issued without a disclosure document under Australian law but qualify for on-sale under the relevant exemption. The company stated it remains compliant with its financial reporting and continuous disclosure obligations and affirmed there is no undisclosed information material to assessing its financial position or the rights attaching to the new shares, aiming to provide regulatory clarity and reassurance to shareholders.

This share issuance notice formalizes secondary trading eligibility for the new Argent BioPharma shares on the Australian market, reducing legal uncertainty for investors who may wish to trade them. By emphasizing its ongoing compliance and the absence of withheld market-sensitive information, the company underlines its governance standards and seeks to maintain confidence as it advances its neuro-immune focused drug pipeline.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RGT) stock is a Hold with a A$0.04 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Argent Biopharma stock, see the AU:RGT Stock Forecast page.

More about Argent Biopharma

Argent BioPharma Ltd. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing nano‑engineered medicines aimed at restoring balance between the nervous and immune systems. Its lead candidates, CannEpil and CimetrA, target drug-resistant epilepsy and cytokine-driven inflammatory and autoimmune disorders, supported by proprietary delivery platforms that enhance blood–brain and alveolar–capillary barrier penetration.

China State Construction Development Holdings Limited ( (HK:0830) ) has provided an update.

China State Construction Development Holdings Limited has announced a proposed final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.01 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval at the company’s annual general meeting on 17 June 2026. Metrics Master Income Trust has reported an unaudited net tangible asset (NTA) backing of $2.0082 per unit as at 19 March 2026, with NTA and NAV treated equivalently for reporting purposes in Australian dollars. The disclosure provides investors with an updated view of the trust’s per‑unit asset value, supporting transparency for unitholders assessing performance and valuation relative to its income and capital‑preservation objectives.

data.title: Neometals re-enters upstream lithium with Utah brine joint venture
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Neometals Ltd ( (AU:NMT) ) has shared an announcement.

Neometals has agreed to acquire a 51% stake in Utah Brine Corporation, a special purpose vehicle holding a large lithium and potash brine project in Utah’s Paradox Basin, by funding up to US$2 million in project costs over three years. The deal marks Neometals’ return to upstream critical minerals and provides low-cost exposure to more than 80,000 acres of prospective brine tenure in the Lisbon Valley area.

Utah Brine Corporation has secured an exclusive access and use licence over 24 inactive oil and gas wells, associated leases and infrastructure, enabling brine sampling, technical test work and project evaluation activities. While no mineral resources or reserves have yet been defined and no development decision has been made, the joint venture positions Neometals to leverage its lithium expertise in a strategic U.S. jurisdiction alongside its 49% partner Omaha Value Inc., which will fund a separate payment to the licence holders upon permitting milestones.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:NMT) stock is a Hold with a A$0.06 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Neometals Ltd stock, see the AU:NMT Stock Forecast page.

More about Neometals Ltd

Neometals Ltd is an Australian critical minerals company with experience across the lithium value chain, including development of the Mt Marion lithium mine in Western Australia and the patented ELi lithium processing technology. The company focuses on upstream and downstream lithium projects and is now seeking to expand its presence in North American brine-hosted lithium and potash opportunities.

Average Trading Volume: 960,281

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$44.69M

data.title: NZME sets April date for AGM as shareholders weigh board refresh
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NZME ( (NZMEF) ) has shared an announcement.

NZME will hold its 2026 Annual Shareholders’ Meeting on 22 April in Auckland and online, giving investors the option of in-person or virtual participation. The agenda includes addresses from chair Steven Joyce and CEO Michael Boggs, alongside votes on director elections, auditor remuneration and other general business.

Shareholders will vote on confirming independent directors Bowen Pan and Kate Parsons to the board, with Parsons expected to become Audit and Risk Committee chair following Carol Campbell’s planned retirement. Investors will also decide on the election of director nominee Benedict Ong and authorise the board to set audit fees, decisions that will shape NZME’s governance, board skill mix and oversight of its financial reporting.

The board has endorsed Pan and Parsons, citing their expertise in digital platforms, AI-driven software and high-growth company finance as aligned with NZME’s strategic needs. While the board has not taken a position on Ong’s candidacy, the outcome of these resolutions will determine the board’s size and composition as NZME pursues its media and digital growth strategy in a competitive New Zealand market.

More about NZME

NZME Limited is a New Zealand media company with operations spanning radio, digital and print, including assets such as iHeart and the OneRoof property platform. The group focuses on news, entertainment and advertising services in the New Zealand market, and is listed on the NZX with an established governance framework overseen by an independent board of directors.

The company continues to strengthen its board with directors experienced in digital platforms, technology and finance, aiming to support its strategy in a rapidly evolving media and advertising landscape. Its governance structure includes an Audit and Risk Committee, reflecting the regulatory and financial oversight requirements typical for a listed media group.

NZME’s board composition and committee leadership are being refreshed to maintain an appropriate mix of skills as the business navigates structural change in media consumption and advertising markets. The addition of directors with global technology, AI, marketplaces and finance backgrounds signals an emphasis on digital transformation and disciplined financial management across its portfolio.

Find detailed analytics on NZMEF stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: GMR Power and Urban Infra Sets Up New Renewable Energy Subsidiary in Andhra Pradesh
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

GMR Power and Urban Infra Ltd ( ($IN:GMRP&UI) ) just unveiled an announcement.

GMR Power and Urban Infra Ltd has announced that its wholly owned subsidiary, GMR Energy Ltd, has incorporated a new step-down subsidiary, GMR Andhra Pradesh Renewable Energy-I Ltd. The new entity is fully owned, has an initial paid-up capital of Rs. 5 lakh and is not categorized as a related-party transaction, with no separate promoter group interest beyond group ownership.

GMR Andhra Pradesh Renewable Energy-I Ltd has been created to explore business opportunities in the renewable energy segment, specifically for power generation and transmission from renewable resources. The company is yet to commence active operations, but the move signals GMR Power and Urban Infra’s intention to expand its footprint in the renewable energy space and potentially strengthen its positioning in India’s evolving clean power market.

More about GMR Power and Urban Infra Ltd

GMR Power and Urban Infra Ltd operates in the power and urban infrastructure sector, with activities spanning energy generation and related services through its subsidiaries. The company, via wholly owned unit GMR Energy Ltd, is increasingly focusing on renewable energy initiatives to diversify its portfolio and align with the growing clean energy market in India.

Cosmo Metals Limited ( (AU:CMO) ) has issued an update.

Cosmo Metals Limited has disclosed a change in the indirect interests of director Ian Prentice, lodged under ASX listing rule obligations. The notice records his holdings through the I&T Prentice Family Account, reflecting updated positions in fully paid ordinary shares, unlisted options, and performance rights.

Prentice acquired 3,749,167 fully paid ordinary shares and 937,293 unlisted options, lifting his shareholding to 8,691,422 shares alongside a substantial suite of options and performance rights. The additional securities were obtained via participation in a retail entitlement offer and shortfall entitlement offer, signalling board-level support for the company’s latest capital raising and potential alignment with shareholder interests.
